purpose: RST AI Provider API interaction contracts. Used to decide which fields may be sent, which fields must be present, and which response fields should be parsed or ignored.

field_status.required: Must be present for this protocol or object shape.
field_status.optional: May be sent when the target model/provider supports it.
field_status.conditional: May or must be sent only when the condition is true.
field_status.forbidden: Do not send for this protocol/provider shape.
field_status.ignored: Provider accepts or tolerates it but documents that it has no effect.

conventions.path_syntax: Dotted paths use [] for array items, such as messages[].content or choices[].message.content.
conventions.implementation_rule: Provider adapters should whitelist fields from allowed_request_fields and conditional_request_fields. Do not forward unknown UI fields directly to remote APIs.

strict_request_whitelist.notes: Must be one of the models enabled by the Coding Plan package.

adapter_policy.notes: Coding Plan is a coding-tool package, not the general Model Studio pay-as-you-go API. Treat OpenAI compatibility as a narrow tool-facing subset unless a selected tool/model explicitly documents more fields.

usage_constraints: Official docs state Coding Plan is limited to AI coding tools/OpenClaw-like agents and must not be used for automated scripts, custom app backends, workflow platforms, or non-interactive batch calls.
Coding Plan API keys are package-specific and use sk-sp-* format; do not mix them with pay-as-you-go Model Studio keys or base URLs.

aliyun_coding_plan_openai.compatibility_notes: Do not treat inherits=openai_chat_completions as permission to forward every OpenAI field. Prefer claude_code_interface when integrating through a Claude Code-like tool surface.

claude_code_interface.purpose: Single Claude Code-compatible interface for Coding Plan packages. Provider variants only define endpoint, model, and environment differences.
claude_code_interface.selection_rule: Expose this as one provider/interface in RST. Select a provider_variant such as aliyun_coding_plan or xfyun_astron_coding_plan from account/package settings.

claude_code.purpose: Emit Anthropic Messages-compatible requests in the same shape used by Claude Code configuration documented by Coding Plan providers.

request_shape.model: Use the selected provider_variant model policy.
request_shape.system: top-level array of text blocks; do not send messages[].role=system
request_shape.messages: array of user/assistant turns only; merge consecutive user turns and keep tool_use/tool_result pairing valid
request_shape.tools: optional Anthropic tool schema array with name, description, and input_schema
request_shape.metadata: optional Claude Code metadata object, typically metadata.user_id JSON string
request_shape.betas: conditional; only provider-accepted Claude Code beta features
request_shape.speed: conditional provider-gated Claude Code fast mode

system.text: You are a concise coding assistant.

claude_code.notes: This means Anthropic Messages wire format and documented Claude Code environment variables, not falsifying client identity.

adapter_policy.notes: This is the canonical Coding Plan interface for Claude Code-like integrations. OpenAI-compatible Coding Plan contracts are fallback compatibility surfaces, not separate first-class providers.

claude_code_interface.compatibility_notes: Use Claude Code Interface as the single Coding Plan provider surface. Provider variants share Anthropic Messages field rules and differ only in endpoint, model policy, environment mapping, and package restrictions.

model_policy.notes: Official Coding Plan doc says all requests should use model=astron-code-latest; underlying model is selected in the MaaS package page and takes 1-3 minutes to apply.

adapter_policy.notes: Although this endpoint is documented as OpenAI protocol, observed compatibility should be treated as a narrow Chat Completions subset. Start from the whitelist and add fields only after provider-specific verification.

forbidden_request_fields.reason: Official Coding Plan doc declares a unified model ID.

forbidden_request_fields.path: base URL for non-Coding MaaS inference
forbidden_request_fields.reason: Coding Plan uses maas-coding-api; do not mix with regular MaaS API endpoints.

forbidden_request_fields.path: full OpenAI Chat Completions parameter passthrough
forbidden_request_fields.reason: This contract deliberately narrows the inherited OpenAI Chat surface; adapters must apply strict_request_whitelist before sending requests.

xfyun_astron_coding_plan_openai.compatibility_notes: Do not treat inherits=openai_chat_completions as permission to forward every OpenAI field. Prefer claude_code_interface for Claude Code-like integrations; this contract remains a narrow OpenAI-compatible fallback.

structured_output_strategy.openai_responses: Use text.format/json_schema or tools where supported.
structured_output_strategy.openai_chat_completions: Use response_format.type=json_schema when supported; otherwise json_object plus schema validation.
structured_output_strategy.anthropic_messages: Use tool use with a schema-bearing virtual tool.
structured_output_strategy.deepseek_chat: Use response_format.type=json_object plus prompt instruction and local schema validation.
structured_output_strategy.aliyun_qwen_openai_chat: Use response_format.type=json_object or documented structured output support, then validate locally.
structured_output_strategy.coding_plan: Prefer claude_code_interface for Claude Code-like integrations. Use OpenAI-compatible Coding Plan contracts only as narrow fallback surfaces. Validate responses locally because Coding Plan docs specify compatibility endpoints rather than full per-field schema.

coding_plan_protocol_selection.default_rule: Do not use general-purpose OpenAI/Anthropic adapters unchanged against Coding Plan endpoints.
coding_plan_protocol_selection.openai_compatible: Use only the provider-specific strict_request_whitelist and avoid full OpenAI Chat parameter passthrough.
coding_plan_protocol_selection.claude_code_like: Use claude_code_interface with a provider_variant, top-level system, user/assistant messages only, max_tokens, and the documented Claude Code environment variables.
coding_plan_protocol_selection.identity_rule: Do not spoof a false User-Agent or client identity. 'Claude Code-compatible' means request shape and documented environment mapping, not pretending to be a different client.

sampling_policy.rule: Expose temperature/top_p but warn users that many providers recommend changing only one at a time.

provider_limits.aliyun_qwen_openai_chat: temperature range is [0,2); top_p range is (0,1].

structured_output_details.summary: Provider-specific mechanisms for constraining model output to strict JSON schemas.

mechanisms.name: output_config.format
mechanisms.description: Native structured output via output_config.format with JSON Schema. SDKs support Pydantic/Zod auto-conversion.

schema_requirements.additionalProperties: Must be false for all objects
schema_requirements.required: List required properties; they appear before optional ones in output

mechanisms.guarantees: Grammar-constrained sampling ensures output matches schema exactly.

mechanisms.name: strict_tool_use
mechanisms.description: Tool definitions with strict:true guarantee tool call parameters match input_schema exactly.

usage.schema_requirements: Same as output_config.format - additionalProperties must be false

mechanisms.guarantees: Tool input strictly follows input_schema; tool name is always valid.

data_retention.phi_warning: Do NOT include PHI in schema definitions (property names, enum values, const, pattern). PHI only in message content.

mechanisms.name: response_format.type=json_object
mechanisms.description: JSON mode guarantees valid JSON output, but NOT schema compliance.

usage.critical_requirement: MUST instruct model to produce JSON via system or user message. Without this, model may generate endless whitespace until token limit.

mechanisms.guarantees: Output is valid JSON. Does NOT guarantee schema compliance.
mechanisms.recommended_practice: Use with prompt instruction specifying desired JSON structure, then validate locally.

mechanisms.name: tools[].strict
mechanisms.description: Function calling with strict mode ensures tool call parameters match JSON schema.
mechanisms.guarantees: Tool call input complies with function's JSON schema when strict=true.

deepseek_chat.recommended_strategy: For structured output: (1) Use response_format.type=json_object with explicit JSON instruction in prompt, (2) Validate response against schema locally, OR (3) Use tools with strict:true for function calling scenarios.

mechanisms.name: response_format.type=json_schema
mechanisms.description: Strict JSON Schema output for Chat Completions API.
mechanisms.guarantees: Output matches schema exactly when using json_schema type.

mechanisms.name: response_format.type=json_object
mechanisms.description: Basic JSON mode - valid JSON but no schema guarantee.

mechanisms.name: text.format
mechanisms.description: Structured output via text.format in Responses API.
